spots.name: Osaka Castle
spots.trivia: Built by Toyotomi Hideyoshi in 1583. One of Japan's most famous landmarks, surrounded by moats and stone walls.

spots.name: Dotonbori
spots.trivia: Osaka's entertainment hub, famous for its neon signs, the Glico Running Man, and endless street food stalls.

spots.name: Shitennoji Temple
spots.trivia: Founded in 593 CE by Prince Shotoku — one of the oldest officially administered temples in Japan.

spots.name: Sumiyoshi Taisha
spots.trivia: One of Japan's oldest shrines, dedicated to sea gods. Its distinctive architecture predates Buddhism's influence.

spots.name: Kuromon Ichiba Market
spots.trivia: Called 'Osaka's Kitchen' — a 600-stall covered market where locals shop for the freshest seafood and produce.

spots.name: Tsutenkaku Tower
spots.trivia: A retro tower in the Shinsekai district. Rebuilt in 1956, it symbolises Osaka's post-war recovery and Billiken.

spots.name: Shinsekai District
spots.trivia: A nostalgic neighbourhood built as 'the new world' in 1912, mixing Parisian and New York styles. Home of kushikatsu.

spots.name: Nakanoshima Park
spots.trivia: A rose garden and park on an island between two rivers in central Osaka. A beloved urban green escape.

spots.name: Osaka Aquarium Kaiyukan
spots.trivia: One of the world's largest aquariums, built around the Pacific Ocean ecosystem and centred on whale sharks.

spots.name: Tempozan Ferris Wheel
spots.trivia: Once the world's largest Ferris wheel. Light-up displays forecast the next day's weather for locals.

spots.name: Tennoji Zoo
spots.trivia: One of Japan's oldest zoos (1915), adjacent to the Tennoji Park gardens and the ancient Shitennoji.

spots.name: Namba Yasaka Shrine
spots.trivia: Famous for its enormous lion-head stage used in traditional Noh and Kagura dance performances.

spots.name: Hozenji Yokocho
spots.trivia: A moss-covered cobblestone alley with tiny restaurants and bars, centred on the moss-covered Fudo Myo-o statue.

spots.name: Expo Commemoration Park
spots.trivia: The site of the 1970 World Expo. Features the iconic Tower of the Sun by Taro Okamoto and vast Japanese gardens.

spots.name: Minoo Park Waterfall
spots.trivia: A forested park north of Osaka famous for its 33-metre waterfall and brilliant autumn maple foliage.

spots.name: Tenjinbashisuji Shotengai
spots.trivia: Japan's longest covered shopping street, stretching 2.6 km with over 600 shops from traditional to modern.

spots.name: Osaka Museum of History
spots.trivia: A 10-storey museum overlooking Osaka Castle with reconstructed Naniwa Palace interiors and cityscape views.

spots.name: Shitenno-ji Haiden
spots.trivia: The main hall of Shitennoji where flea markets are held on the 21st and 22nd of each month in the temple precincts.

spots.name: Tsuyu no Ten Shrine
spots.trivia: The love shrine near Sonezaki — dedicated to Ohatsu and Tokubei, Japan's most famous tragic lovers.

spots.name: Namba Grand Kagetsu
spots.trivia: Osaka's legendary comedy theatre, home of the Yoshimoto manzai comedy tradition since 1912.
